evidence: We currently incorporate machine learning and AI capabilities into certain of our products and solutions and may seek to expand the use of AI in our offerings in the future.
Some of our product sales are tied to artificial intelligence (AI)-related capital expenditures and data center infrastructure.
The rapid evolution of AI will require the application of resources by us to develop, test and maintain our products, services and operations.
We currently incorporate machine learning and AI capabilities into certain of our products and solutions and may seek to expand the use of AI in our offerings in the future.
Some of our product sales are tied to artificial intelligence (AI)-related capital expenditures and data center infrastructure.
leveraging AI capabilities to potentially improve internal functions and operations presents further risks and challenges.

AI algorithms and training methodologies may be flawed. These deficiencies and other failures of AI systems could subject us to competitive harm, regulatory action, legal liability, and brand or reputational harm.

incorporating AI could give rise to litigation risk and risk of non-compliance and unknown cost of compliance, as AI is an emerging technology for which the legal and regulatory landscape is not fully developed.
our competitors might move faster than us to gain efficiencies by incorporating AI into their design and development processes, and our products and/or cost structure could become less competitive.

limitations: No quantified AI-attributable dollar amounts, headcount, or capex figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity capped at 3.
AI use cases in products are named (machine learning/AI in products and solutions) but no deployment metrics or quantified business results are disclosed; concreteness capped at 3.

Risk disclosure is company-specific with named mechanisms (competitive harm, regulatory action, data privacy, IP liability) but no quantified risk figures or formally named AI governance body beyond the general Audit Committee cybersecurity oversight; scored 4 rather than 5.
